meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding to fix debugging hurdles. A beginner's guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional, error-free SaaS applications.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's about context. By integrating structured user onboarding, you force yourself to define exact data flows and states. This clarity eliminates ambiguity, allowing AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to pinpoint logic errors instantly. Stop guessing—start engineering robust, verifiable software systems today.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. Your code is a sprawling web of logic that seemed perfect in your head, but now it’s just throwing cryptic errors you can’t unravel. The frustration of being unable to debug isn't a lack of talent—it's a lack of structure in your application's lifecycle.

Great engineering begins with the user's journey. When you build a rigorous onboarding flow, you aren't just helping the user; you are creating a sandbox that maps exactly how data enters and behaves in your app. By mastering this, you transform from a struggling student into an architect who builds systems that are inherently easier to troubleshoot.

steps.title: Map the User State Machine
steps.description: Define the exact states a user passes through during onboarding to prevent logic leaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current user onboarding flow and generate a state machine diagram that identifies potential null-state errors in my authentication logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map user journey and identify potential state transition bugs'

steps.title: Implement Instrumented Onboarding
steps.description: Add logging at every onboarding milestone to trace data flow failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my onboarding middleware to include structured logging for every user input step, ensuring data integrity is verifiable.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/onboarding.js --instruction 'inject logging for state transitions'

steps.title: Automate Data Validation
steps.description: Use schema enforcement to catch bugs before they hit the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Zod validation schema for my onboarding form that catches common data type mismatches before they reach the backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate Zod schemas for all onboarding inputs'

steps.title: Simulate Edge-Case Onboarding
steps.description: Use AI to stress-test your onboarding flow against invalid or malicious inputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a QA engineer and simulate 5 edge-case user onboarding scenarios that could break my current codebase.

steps.title: Refactor to Modular Components
steps.description: Break down monolith onboarding code into testable, isolated modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose my monolithic onboarding component into smaller, testable functional modules to improve maintainability and debuggability.

pro_tips: Treat every user error as a missing unit test; if a user can break it, your code is missing a validation layer.
Use 'dry-run' flags in your onboarding database calls to debug flows without polluting your production environment.
Implement a 'rollback' state in your onboarding logic so users can recover when things crash, preventing data corruption.
Leverage Claude Code's memory feature to keep track of previous debugging sessions across your entire codebase.
Documentation is code—if you can't explain your onboarding flow in plain English, your code is too complex to be debugged.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable, move on to building a custom dashboard that visualizes your application's health metrics in real-time.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop fighting your code and start mastering it. The difference between a hobbyist and a developer is the ability to build systems that scale and self-report errors. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
